mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% indicates deep easing regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5% do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 9.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 9.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 600k units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 575k units indicates weak housing demand
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction with synchronized demand weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint RSAFS and HOUST weakness, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 101.0 signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 101.0 for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.61 near recent support levels
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO < 101.0 do not show significant negative bias (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals synchronized labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100k month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 129.75M and UNRATE at 9.7% indicate labor stress
regime_hint: labor market recession with synchronized employment and unemployment deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite joint employment decline and unemployment rise, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 220.0 combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 1.0%) signals monetary tightening that increases real borrowing costs and reduces present value of future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.73 near threshold, FEDFUNDS at 0.16% currently low
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both CPIAUCSL > 220.0 and FEDFUNDS > 1.0%, or interaction coefficient fails to reach statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter shows a sustained decline below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle economic deceleration that reduces earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13149.5B represents baseline for growth assessment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating growth momentum
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-growth quarters do not show significant negative bias compared to high-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 218.0 signals monetary policy constraint and reduced real money supply, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above 218.0 for two consecutive months
regime_hint: inflationary environment with monetary policy tightening bias
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-inflation periods (CPIAUCSL > 218.0) do not show significant negative bias (t-test p > 0.05).
